|timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]<ref name="Date note">The events of ''Star Wars'' 15 take place after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and prior to the events of [[Star Wars (1977) 25|''Star Wars'' (1977) 25]], which include the [[Yavin blockade]]. Since [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] dates both the Battle of Yavin and the Yavin blockade to [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the events of ''Star Wars'' 15 must also occur in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]].</ref>
|series=[[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'' (1977)]]
|issue=15
}}
'''''Star Wars'' 15''' is the fifteenth issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']]. It was written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]] and [[Terry Austin]], and first published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[June 27]], [[1978]]. It features the story "'''Star Duel!'''," in which [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] faces off against the [[Pirate/Legends|space pirate]] [[Crimson Jack/Legends|Crimson Jack]].

==Publisher's summary==
''When the ''Millenium Falcon'' [''sic''] is disabled, Han will have to outduel space pirates to survive!''

==Plot summary==
With [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Quarg]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] and [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[Jammer/Legends|jamming equipment]] disabled, [[Crimson Jack/Legends|Crimson Jack's]] [[Crimson Jack's battlecruiser|battlecruiser]] is fully operational and waiting for Han Solo and the ''Millennium Falcon'' to leave the surface of [[Drexel/Legends|Drexel]] so Jack can exact some revenge. [[Jolli]] engages in a [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]] with the ''Falcon'' which results in her [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] and the ''Falcon'' becoming disabled. Although Solo appears helpless as a result of the damage, he forces a personal exchange between himself and Jack when he reveals that [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] erased Jack's [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigational charts]] while on board his [[Battlecruiser/Legends|cruiser]]. Meanwhile, Jolli requests assistance because of damage sustained in the earlier battle, but Jack dismisses her, focusing instead on how to get back at Solo. Angered by Jack's response, Jolli fires on her former allies and rams her [[Starship/Legends|ship]] into the battlecruiser, killing the crew and herself. While Jack survives the blast, he is still bent on killing Solo, but in the final duel between the two, Solo ends up killing the [[Color/Legends|red]]-[[Hair/Legends|bearded]] pirate with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].

==Continuity==
In ''Star Wars'' 15, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] claims that he does not know how to swim, while [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] is able to. In the previously published novel ''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]'' ([[1978]]), the reverse is true.<ref name="Splinter of the Mind's Eye">''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]''</ref>
